description.value:
Used as a replacement for skills rolled with base COOL, EMP, or WILL

Used for rolling in checks which involve force of will, charisma, general intelligence, or intution. Note that if the check is specialized and the mook is not, the DV should be raised 1-3 tiers.

Used as a replacement for fighting or ranged weapon skills, excluding evasion. Note that this uses REF as a base for reasons involving initiative - set REF of the mook first, then consider that number when considering the Martial skill.
Used as a replacement for skills which rely on base Body or Dex, which are not covered by the Martial skill, and for Evasion.
Used for skills which rely on base INT or TECH.
A scavenged set of Light Armorjack body armor that is unable to be repaired. Refer to full item description on BC+ pg. 4.
A very heavy pistol. It can't be concealed.

Generic Poor Quality Medium Melee Weapon.

If you roll 1 on Attack (Crit Fail) the weapon fails in some way and requires an action to be made usable again.

information.notes:
Mook Tier - Tier 4/Elite Threat - Mid-level, named threats, who run the bulk of activity for most orgs within NC. If a PC didn't choose to become an edgerunner, they probably would've eventually ended up in a position like this instead.

Gang-Wide Ability - Ambush!: Raffen strike fast and hard. When Raffen roll initiative in an encounter, their vehicles roll initiative for their entire occupancy, and always have 8 REF for purposes of doing so. Vehicles can immediately be placed anywhere on the map that they would be able to reach without moving through other characters, regardless of movement. At the start of the first round of combat, choose 1 vehicle (2 if your party count numbers 6 or more); that vehicle(s) and their occupants always go first for that round.

Ability - Bandit Moto: When a driver enters the scene, they can do so with a vehicle. Choose between a @Compendium[night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsItems22.32HPQiSeW4RLZs2g]{Scrap Gyro}, a @Actor[tgd7ch4SfnbkpO9z]{Raffen Technical}, a @Actor[FMLgFsoNpK63iGNI]{Raffen Buggy}, a @Compendium[night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsItems22.x9phBEbuufeWlZiW]{Motorcycle}, a @Actor[bheOO3FzXxdlmUsC]{Raffen Hauler} or if on water, a @Compendium[cyberpunk-red-core.core_vehicles.x0E0hYFqfP3qJGPT]{Jetski}, @Compendium[cyberpunk-red-core.core_vehicles.eKqDVQo3mzD16pCc]{Speedboat} or @Compendium[night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsItems22.a3n93Usql4QNGt71]{RHIB Boat}. They can drive the vehicle one-handed, and receive +1 to their rolls while doing so.

You may not like scavs, but they stay in their corners and the NCPD handles them. There's only one group in the world that Militech is contracted to hunt down, whether the rest of the nomads like it or not - but if they'd done their dirty laundry, the Raffen wouldn't exist, eh? Word of advice - keep one in the chamber while you're out in the desert. Don't let them take you alive.
